ac_047_interpretation: option-2 — spec-intent: the AC's second clause was mis-written. The spec's ## Design section explicitly states that receivers ignore the outer rumor.pubkey for profile rumors and rely solely on the embedded SignedProfileEvent.sig as the trust anchor. A malicious member cannot forge signedEvent.pubkey for another author without producing an invalid sig, so verifyEvent() in parseProfilePayload is the correct and sufficient guard. The outer/inner pubkey mismatch condition describes legitimate relay-on-behalf, which the spec endorses. Dropping on that condition would break story 06 entirely. AC-047 second clause was amended to specify: drop when verifyEvent(signedEvent) is false; cross-pubkey relay must be accepted.
ac_039_fix: Removed spurious 'await saveMemberProfiles(groupId, existing)' from the LWW no-update branch in mergeMemberProfile (groupStorage.ts line 157); branch now returns false immediately with zero IDB writes.
notes: The reconciliation report's AC-047 verdict ('silent identity confusion') was based on a literal reading of the AC text that contradicts the spec's design rationale. The correct fix is a spec amendment, not a code change. The implementation already behaves correctly per the spec's intent. Both reconciliation.json verdicts now show 'holds'.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: AC-047's second clause contained a contradiction with the spec's own ## Design section — the literal AC text would break the core relay-on-behalf feature that the entire epic exists to implement. The reconciliation report flagged this as a code defect ('silent identity confusion') when it was actually a spec authoring error. Resolving this required careful cross-reading of three documents (AC, spec, reconciliation) before any code could be touched.
retrospective.surprised_by: The spurious saveMemberProfiles call in the no-update branch (Defect 2) was a single-line removal that the test suite immediately confirmed — no subtlety there. The asymmetry in defect complexity (Defect 1 required spec-level reasoning, Defect 2 required one line deleted) means the reconciliation severity scores (~7 vs ~4) undersell how much cognitive work Defect 1 demanded relative to its zero-code-change resolution.
